---
title: "DS2020 Final Prespoal"
author: "Scott Engstrom"
date: "2026-04-09"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:



```{r}
# Load packages
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(tidyverse)
library(rlang)
#install.packages("openxlsx")
library(openxlsx)   # for Excel export

# Your API key
api_key <- "Ki8M5hjyUxGEPrU2OwWXqUK9pYvDuE9m42ujQ9dt"

# Max allowed by NASA API
page_size <- 20  

# -------------------------------
# STEP 1: Get total number of pages
# -------------------------------
url_total <- paste0(
  "https://api.nasa.gov/neo/rest/v1/neo/browse?",
  "api_key=", api_key,
  "&page=0&size=1"
)

res_total <- GET(url_total)

if (res_total$status_code != 200) {
  stop("Failed to get total pages")
}

data_total <- fromJSON(rawToChar(res_total$content), simplifyDataFrame = FALSE)
total_pages <- data_total$page$total_pages

cat("Total pages:", total_pages, "\n")

# -------------------------------
# STEP 2: Function to pull one page
# -------------------------------
get_neo_page <- function(page_num) {
  
  url <- paste0(
    "https://api.nasa.gov/neo/rest/v1/neo/browse?",
    "api_key=", api_key,
    "&page=", page_num,
    "&size=", page_size
  )
  
  res <- GET(url)
  
  if (res$status_code != 200) {
    warning("Failed page ", page_num)
    return(tibble())
  }
  
  data <- fromJSON(rawToChar(res$content), simplifyDataFrame = FALSE)
  neo_list <- data$near_earth_objects
  
  if (length(neo_list) == 0) return(tibble())
  
  tibble(
    neo_reference_id = map_chr(neo_list, ~ .x$neo_reference_id %||% NA_character_),
    name = map_chr(neo_list, ~ .x$name %||% NA_character_),
    absolute_magnitude_h = map_dbl(neo_list, ~ .x$absolute_magnitude_h %||% NA_real_),
    
    est_diam_min_km = map_dbl(
      neo_list,
      ~ .x$estimated_diameter$kilometers$estimated_diameter_min %||% NA_real_
    ),
    
    est_diam_max_km = map_dbl(
      neo_list,
      ~ .x$estimated_diameter$kilometers$estimated_diameter_max %||% NA_real_
    ),
    
    miss_distance_km = map_dbl(neo_list, function(x) {
      if (length(x$close_approach_data) == 0) return(NA_real_)
      as.numeric(x$close_approach_data[[1]]$miss_distance$kilometers)
    }),
    
    relative_velocity_km_s = map_dbl(neo_list, function(x) {
      if (length(x$close_approach_data) == 0) return(NA_real_)
      as.numeric(x$close_approach_data[[1]]$relative_velocity$kilometers_per_second)
    }),
    
    orbital_eccentricity = map_dbl(
      neo_list,
      ~ as.numeric(.x$orbital_data$eccentricity %||% NA_real_)
    ),
    
    orbital_inclination = map_dbl(
      neo_list,
      ~ as.numeric(.x$orbital_data$inclination %||% NA_real_)
    ),
    
    orbital_semi_major_axis = map_dbl(
      neo_list,
      ~ as.numeric(.x$orbital_data$semi_major_axis %||% NA_real_)
    ),
    
    is_potentially_hazardous_asteroid = map_lgl(
      neo_list,
      ~ .x$is_potentially_hazardous_asteroid %||% FALSE
    )
  )
}

# -------------------------------
# STEP 3: Pull ALL pages
# -------------------------------
all_neo <- map_df(0:(total_pages - 1), function(i) {
  cat("Pulling page", i + 1, "of", total_pages, "\n")
  Sys.sleep(0.2)  # avoid rate limits
  get_neo_page(i)
})

# -------------------------------
# STEP 4: Inspect
# -------------------------------
glimpse(all_neo)

# -------------------------------
# STEP 5: Export to Excel
# -------------------------------
write.xlsx(all_neo, "neo_full_dataset.xlsx")

cat("✅ Export complete: neo_full_dataset.xlsx\n")
```
